Video.summary: If you had a chance to close Shawn, could you get it done? Today's the day we see who has the goods. Each team has one goal. Get the contract signed. Shawn is not going to make it easy, but often your audience isn't the hardest part. Sometimes getting out of your own way is the biggest challenge. Prepare to witness a pitch fest for the ages. You'll see that it doesn't matter what you're selling - a Yacht, a Ferrari, a Private Jet...or a Potato - if you don't connect with Shawn, you have no chance.
Video.title: Millionaire Maker - Punch Me In The Face

Image.alt: Millionaire Maker - Punch Me In The Face
Millionaire Maker - Punch Me In The Face
Millionaire Maker - Punch Me In The Face

Video.summary: This is it. A winner will be chosen. The decision is FINAL...But we still have a crowd of candidates that each think they will be the one. Now comes a test of mental toughness. It's all come down to these final moments. Who can convince Shawn that they won't give up when things get...painful. What would you endure for a chance at greatness?...Watch the season finale of Shawn Meaike's Millionaire Maker.
Video.title: Millionaire Maker - No Time For Cold Feet

Image.alt: Millionaire Maker - No Time For Cold Feet
Millionaire Maker - No Time For Cold Feet
Millionaire Maker - No Time For Cold Feet

Video.summary: Day 4 of Teaching the Teachers focuses on learning to hear the “small still voice for God” through prayer and meditation. It also explores spiritual guidance as an art—meeting others where they are, honoring intuitive limits, and teaching without reinforcing ego or victimhood.
Video.grandparentTitle: Teaching the Teachers with Marianne Williamson
Video.title: Teaching the Teachers - Day 4

Video.summary: Day 5 of Teaching the Teachers reveals that spiritual health and mental health are one. Suffering comes from thinking apart from love, while healing comes through forgiveness and atonement. The role of therapist or teacher is to help remove the barriers to love’s presence.
Video.grandparentTitle: Teaching the Teachers with Marianne Williamson
Video.title: Teaching the Teachers - Day 5

Video.summary: Day 3 focuses on meeting anger and fear with compassion rather than resistance. By paying gentle attention to difficult emotions, we learn they are not bad—only painful when they define us. Using the breath as an anchor, we develop a kinder, wiser relationship to our inner experience.
Video.title: Day 3 - Compassion

Video.summary: Day 4 explores opening to joy by intentionally taking in the good. We examine habits of threat, perfectionism, and resistance, and practice savoring what’s nourishing and life-giving. Through gentle curiosity, this lesson shows how joy can restore resilience and help us meet life with more ease.
Video.title: Day 4 - Open to Joy

Video.summary: Day 5 introduces the practice of lovingkindness, or metta—a meditation rooted in connection and care. Using gentle phrases, we affirm our shared humanity and inner potential for growth and love. This practice invites curiosity rather than force, allowing experience to unfold naturally.
Video.title: Day 5 - Lovingkindness

Video.summary: Did you know being active in the gym could improve your activity in the bedroom? Everything you wanted to ask about sex but didn’t. Now you can on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.
Video.title: Sex Talk - The Sexual Benefits of Working Out

Image.alt: Sex Talk - The Sexual Benefits of Working Out
Sex Talk - The Sexual Benefits of Working Out
Sex Talk - The Sexual Benefits of Working Out

Video.summary: A man gets terrible headaches right at the height of passion. And a woman won’t talk about her sexual desires. What should her partner do? All this and more on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.
Video.title: Sex Talk - It’s Alright to Talk About Sex

Image.alt: Sex Talk - It’s Alright to Talk About Sex
Sex Talk - It’s Alright to Talk About Sex
Sex Talk - It’s Alright to Talk About Sex

Video.summary: Host LisaGarr interviews Marci Shimoff , No. 1 NYT bestselling author, world-renowned transformational teacher, and an expert on happiness, success, and unconditional love, and Dr. Sue Morter, international speaker, Master of Bio-Energetic Medicine and Quantum Field visionary. Marci is the founder of Your Year of Miracles, a yearly online program with Dr. Sue and Lisa Garr in which they discuss the numerous ways of living a truly miraculous life
Video.title: The Aware Show: Marci Shimoff and Dr. Sue Morter - Your Year of Miracles 2022

Image.alt: The Aware Show: Marci Shimoff and Dr. Sue Morter - Your Year of Miracles 2022
The Aware Show: Marci Shimoff and Dr. Sue Morter - Your Year of Miracles 2022
The Aware Show: Marci Shimoff and Dr. Sue Morter - Your Year of Miracles 2022

Video.summary: Host Lisa Garr interviews Kimberly Meredith, spiritual medium and medical intuitive healer who explains the healing process in detail. After a near-death experience, she reawakened her childhood gifts of healing and was able to access dimensions and frequencies higher than what we normally experience in our everyday 3-D world. She talks about living in a limitless reality where all things, including miraculous healings, are possible.
Video.title: The Aware Show: Kimberly Meredith - What is Multi-dimensional Healing?

Image.alt: The Aware Show: Kimberly Meredith - What is Multi-dimensional Healing?
The Aware Show: Kimberly Meredith - What is Multi-dimensional Healing?
The Aware Show: Kimberly Meredith - What is Multi-dimensional Healing?

Video.summary: Host Lisa Garr interviews Bob Roth, one of the most experienced and sought-after meditation leaders in America. For more than 45 years, Bob has taught Transcendental Meditation to many thousands of people, and is the author of the 2018 NYT bestseller, Strength in Stillness: The Power of Transcendental Meditation. Bob talks about the difference between meditation and mindfulness and breaks down the various aspects of exactly how our brainwaves change during the meditation process.
Video.title: The Aware Show: Bob Roth - What is TM?

Image.alt: The Aware Show: Bob Roth - What is TM?
The Aware Show: Bob Roth - What is TM?
The Aware Show: Bob Roth - What is TM?

Video.summary: Host Lisa Garr interviews Terri Cole, licensed psychotherapist, who talks about how to have healthy boundaries and thereby much more balance in your life. She shares ways to set healthy boundaries in order to have your real voice and feelings be heard. She is a global relationship and empowerment expert, and the author of Boundary Boss-The Essential Guide to Talk True, Be Seen and (Finally) Live Free.
Video.title: The Aware Show: Terri Cole - How to Set Healthy Boundaries!

Image.alt: The Aware Show: Terri Cole - How to Set Healthy Boundaries!
The Aware Show: Terri Cole - How to Set Healthy Boundaries!
The Aware Show: Terri Cole - How to Set Healthy Boundaries!

Video.summary: In our medical technology episode, we explore how patients can use mobile apps to monitor and treat chronic conditions, discuss the positive and negative effects of video games on children, and look at how robotic pets are being used to assist people with dementia.
Video.title: Inside the Latest Health Technology

Video.summary: In this episode, we are joined by members of the National Action Alliance for Suicide Prevention to talk about the 988 suicide and crisis lifeline, discussing how the lifeline works and what more needs to be done to ensure equitable support for everyone.
Video.title: What You Need to Know about the 988 Hotline

Image.alt: What You Need to Know about the 988 Hotline
What You Need to Know about the 988 Hotline
What You Need to Know about the 988 Hotline

Video.summary: The president of the American College of Cardiology joins us to discuss how heart disease is the leading cause of death in American women, and how different factors and treatments can lower or worsen your risk of cardiovascular disease.
Video.title: Fighting Heart Disease

Video.summary: John Assaraf, with cameo appearances by with cameo appearances by David Meltzer, Cynthia Kersey, Blaine Bartlett, and emcee, Paisley Demby.
Video.title: World's Greatest Motivators - John Assaraf

Video.summary: Your Day 1 practice offers an invitation to slow down and take stock.
Video.title: Yoga For All Day 1: Notice

Video.summary: Your Day 2 session invites you to use the tools of the at-home yoga practice to befriend your body and your breath. Build on the art of noticing from Day 1 as we expand our vocabulary with a focus on a strong foundation. This session encourages a healthy flow of energy up and down the spine and lovingly guides you to learn how to take responsibility for your experience on the mat.
Video.title: Day 2: Befriend

Video.summary: Your day 3 practice invites you to get to know your body through the structure of the practice and the art of aligning. We build on day 1 and 2 with a focus on action and alignment through asana and energy.
Video.title: Day 3: Align

Video.summary: Your Day 4 practice provides you the opportunity to lean in when you feel discomfort to embrace the process of finding ease. Discover layers of your home practice as we look at our patterns and continue to build a dynamic vocabulary that serves.
Video.title: Day 4: Ease

Video.summary: In our very first solo episode of Rooted in Wellness, we explore the transformative power of being anchored like a sturdy tree—steady, grounded, and unwavering amidst life's storms. We'll illuminate how feeling rooted manifests as inner peace, emotional stability, and a profound connection with yourself and the world around you.
Video.title: 1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness

Image.alt: 1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness
1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness
1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness

Video.summary: Have you ever felt like your hormones are playing a tune out of sync with your well-being? Chances are, they probably are. The good news? There's a transformative journey waiting for women when they realign with nature, pay attention to their meals, and practice intermittent fasting the right way.
Video.title: Dr. Mindy Pelz: How to Become Hormonally Literate through Ancient Healing Practices

Image.alt: Dr. Mindy Pelz: How to Become Hormonally Literate through Ancient Healing Practices
Dr. Mindy Pelz: How to Become Hormonally Literate through Ancient Healing Practices
Dr. Mindy Pelz: How to Become Hormonally Literate through Ancient Healing Practices

Video.summary: Did you know that your metabolic health and energy are controlled by our modern food? This means your metabolism is not broken! You just need to work on healing your metabolism through simple lifestyle shifts you can easily apply to your life today.How? This is what we dive into in this episode of Rooted in Wellness with Casey Means.
Video.title: Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom

Image.alt: Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom
Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom
Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom

Video.summary: A film about Christopher Kirkley’s project Sahel Sounds, which celebrates musical performances of current artists from Niger, while leaving room to reflect on today’s role of ethnomusicology.
Video.title: A STORY OF SAHEL SOUNDS

Video.summary: Fourteen-year-old Mackenzie, a troubled teen, is sent by her mother to live with her uncle in Juneau, Alaska. She runs away and an unlikely friendship forms in the Alaskan wilderness, giving a runaway girl hope and sanctuary in America's last frontier.
Video.title: Episode 1
